mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 2.0 percentage points signals a severe labor market shock that immediately reduces consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during high-volatility periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in residential construction spending and housing starts indicates synchronized private sector demand recovery, which amplifies equity market optimism and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of accommodative monetary policy reflects supply-side capacity utilization improvements that boost short-term equity momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS is above 2.0%, or the mechanism shows negative predictive accuracy during monetary tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.01 across multiple business cycles, or returns are consistently negative during GDP expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation rate exceeding 0.5% triggers expectations of central bank tightening and margin compression, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.5%, or the threshold fails to separate positive and negative return regimes with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power when both series move in the same direction, or out-of-sample tests show no incremental value over single-series models with p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts expansion during periods of low mortgage rates signals durable consumer confidence and wealth effects that translate into positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 60-day returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, or the signal reverses direction during rate hiking cycles with negative Rank IC.
